reasoning: The question asks if there will be >25 riots in the 30-day periods preceding four resolution dates. The threshold is fixed based on a reference window ending today (2025-08-17), meaning ~25 riots are required for any of the periods to resolve YES. This corresponds to an extremely intense wave of unrest (nearly 1 riot per day), similar to the scale seen during the 2009 general strike or the 2021 COVID-19 mandate protests. Extensive web searches in both English and French reveal absolutely no reports of ongoing riots, mass protests, or escalating civil tensions in Guadeloupe in July or August 2025. Given that 23 days have already elapsed for the first resolution date (2025-08-24) with zero major unrest, it is virtually impossible to hit the threshold. For the subsequent dates, we revert to the generic base rate of a severe crisis breaking out in any given month, which historically happens around once every 5 to 10 years (an implied monthly base rate of roughly 1-2%). Therefore, the probabilities are set at the minimal allowed bound (0.05) for the near-term dates and adjusted slightly upward to reflect increasing uncertainty over longer time horizons.
